channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: Ukraine develops first homegrown guided aerial bomb
item.description: Ukraine has declared its domestically-produced 250kg guided aerial bomb combat-ready, reducing dependence on Western precision strike capabilities and providing an answer to Russia's daily glide-bomb attacks.

item.title: Putin-Xi summit to advance Russia-China ties amid Iran war
item.description: Beijing becomes a global focal point as Russia pins hopes on the upcoming Putin-Xi summit, with the Power of Siberia 2 gas pipeline expected to be discussed in detail.

item.title: US extends Russian oil sanctions waiver to aid nations hit by Iran war
item.description: The US Treasury announced another 30-day extension of sanctions waiver allowing purchases of Russian oil, helping countries most affected by disruptions from the Iran conflict.

item.title: South Korea weighs emergency powers to halt Samsung worker strike
item.description: Seoul is considering using rarely invoked emergency powers to stop a Samsung workers' strike that could cost the economy $67 billion, as workers demand $400,000 bonuses.

item.title: US sanctions senior Cuban officials as tensions escalate
item.description: The US sanctioned 11 people including top Cuban officials, generals and intelligence officers, targeting the island's justice ministry and other key figures amid rising tensions.

item.title: Israel-Hezbollah Fighting Continues Despite Extended Truce
item.description: Israel conducted airstrikes in southern Lebanon while Hezbollah announced new attacks on Israeli forces, with the death toll in Lebanon surpassing 3,000. The violence persists despite a U.S.-backed truce extension.

item.title: Polish PM Stresses Transatlantic Unity After U.S. Troop Deployment Cancelled
item.description: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk emphasized the importance of transatlantic ties after the Pentagon cancelled plans to deploy 4,000 U.S.-based troops to Poland, saying the alliance must survive the current difficult period.

item.title: Iran War Tests Dollar-Dominated Oil Trading System
item.description: The global oil trading system centered on the U.S. dollar is being tested by the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz closure, as major consuming nations turn to increasingly opaque deals with Tehran and Gulf producers to secure supplies.

item.title: Trump Called Off Planned Tuesday Attack on Iran, Citing Pending Deal
item.description: President Trump announced he postponed planned attacks on Iran at the request of Gulf allies. Iran said it is not intimidated by threats but talks continue to end the conflict.

item.title: Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant Reaching Point of No Return, Rosatom Warns
item.description: The head of Russia state nuclear corporation Rosatom warned that increased Ukrainian attacks near the Russian-held Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant are pushing the situation to a critical point, raising fears of a nuclear catastrophe in southeastern Ukraine.

item.title: Iran War Costs Global Companies Over $25 Billion and Counting
item.description: Businesses worldwide are grappling with soaring energy prices, fractured supply chains, and severed trade routes as Iran maintains its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz, with the economic toll mounting daily.

item.title: US Extends Russian Oil Sanctions Waiver Amid Iran War Disruption
item.description: The US Treasury announced a 30-day extension of sanctions waivers allowing purchases of Russian seaborne oil to help energy-vulnerable countries cope with supply disruptions caused by the Iran conflict.

item.title: Global Bond Rout Deepens as Inflation Fears Mount
item.description: Sovereign bond yields are racing to decade highs as investors wake up to the risk that the Iran war may deliver a lasting inflationary shock, threatening government, business, and household spending power worldwide.

item.title: Iran Creates New Body to Manage Strait of Hormuz as Talks Stall
item.description: Iran announced a new oversight body for the strategic Strait of Hormuz waterway, signaling tighter control as peace talks with the US remain deadlocked and the risk of renewed conflict rises.

item.title: Cuba warns of bloodbath if US attacks as Washington imposes sanctions
item.description: President Diaz-Canel warned of a bloodbath if the US attacks Cuba, while the US Treasury sanctioned Cuba's main intelligence agency and top leaders amid reports Havana obtained military drones from Russia and Iran.

item.title: Ten countries condemn Israel's interception of Gaza humanitarian flotilla
item.description: Turkey and nine other countries issued a strong condemnation of Israel's attack on a humanitarian flotilla attempting to reach Gaza, calling it a violation of international law.

item.title: US asks Israel to keep refueling aircraft at Ben Gurion through 2026
item.description: The US has requested Israel to keep its refueling aircraft at Ben Gurion Airport until the end of 2026, with reports indicating the presence of US aircraft is causing operational difficulties at the airport.

item.title: Fifty countries back Taiwan's WHA participation bid despite Chinese opposition
item.description: Over 50 countries have expressed support for Taiwan's participation in the World Health Assembly and other international organizations, while the WHO rejected a proposal to invite Taiwan after China vowed to block participation.

item.title: Philippine Senate presidency fight could shape Sara Duterte impeachment trial
item.description: The Philippine Senate convened as an impeachment court for Vice-President Sara Duterte amid a leadership battle that could influence her trial and the fallout from her father's ICC case.

item.title: Pakistan Deploys Fighter Jets and 8,000 Troops to Saudi Arabia Amid Iran War
item.description: Pakistan has stationed a fighter squadron and thousands of soldiers in Saudi Arabia during the Iran conflict, deepening military cooperation with Riyadh while simultaneously mediating between Tehran and Washington.

item.title: Greenland Says US Talks Make Progress But Island Is Not For Sale
item.description: Greenlands government reports progress in high-stakes talks with the United States over the territorys future, while firmly rejecting any possibility of sale despite President Trumps persistent interest in acquiring the strategic Arctic territory.

item.title: Russian Forces Strike Odesa and Dnipro With Missiles and Drones
item.description: Russia attacked the Ukrainian cities of Odesa and Dnipro with missiles and drones, damaging residential buildings and injuring dozens, while Ukrainian forces struck the Belgorod region, killing two people according to Russian officials.

item.title: France's Macron Kicks Off Kenya Visit for Africa Forward Summit
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron began a visit to Kenya ahead of the Africa Forward Summit, showcasing France's new policy for the continent - a shift from colonial power to partnership of equals amid changing global geopolitics.

item.title: Hamas Military Wing Leader Killed in Israeli Airstrike
item.description: Israel confirmed killing Izz al-Din al-Haddad, one of the last surviving architects of the October 7 attacks, in a Gaza airstrike. Hamas confirmed the death amid an ongoing fragile ceasefire.

item.title: First Russian Student Recruited From University Dies in Ukraine
item.description: Valery Averin, 23, became the first known Russian recruited directly from a university to die in Ukraine, exposing Moscow's campaign to recruit students into military service through financial incentives.
